name: Genability Electricity Rate and Tariff API
what_it_does: Electricity rate and tariff data API — enabling solar, energy storage, EV charging, and utility analytics companies to access structured rate data for 5,000+ US electric utilities, calculate electricity bills under different tariff scenarios, model demand charges, TOU rates, and EV charging costs, and optimize energy decisions based on accurate utility rate intelligence.
use_cases: Retrieving utility rate structures via Genability API for solar payback analysis
Calculating electricity bills under time-of-use tariffs via Genability for energy optimization
Modeling demand charge exposure via Genability API for commercial C&I energy storage
Fetching EV charging tariff data via Genability for fleet electrification cost analysis
Comparing utility tariff options via Genability API for retail energy advisory services
not_for: Real-time electricity pricing (Genability has tariff data, not real-time spot prices)
International markets (Genability is US-focused utility coverage)
Utility meter data access (use Green Button / UtilityAPI for interval data)
best_when: A solar, storage, EV, or energy analytics company needs accurate, structured US utility rate data — where Genability provides the authoritative tariff database for calculating bills, payback periods, and demand charges across thousands of utilities.
avoid_when: You need real-time market prices, international utility data, or actual meter interval data from customers.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth using App ID (username) and App Key (password). Base64-encoded credentials in Authorization header. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Keys generated via Genability developer portal. JavaScript, Python, and Java SDK clients available. No webhooks — REST pull model only.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free sandbox with limited API calls for development
pricing.paid_tiers: Production plans by API call volume; Enterprise custom pricing
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2009. Raised $8M+ total funding. Used by solar installers, utilities, energy companies, EV charging networks. 5,000+ US electric utilities tracked. Tariff data manually curated by Genability analysts. Solar payback calculators built on Genability. Customers: SunPower, SolarCity (Tesla), Enphase, ChargePoint. Acquired tariff data business from OpenEI partnership. US-only coverage. Rate data updated as utilities file tariff changes. Private LSEID (load serving entity ID) for each utility. Bill calculation API handles complex TOU/tiered/demand structures.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; sandbox limits apply to free tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with HTTP Basic auth (App ID + App Key). JS/Python/Java SDKs. No webhooks. US-only coverage. Good developer documentation. Tariff calculation API handles complex rate structures. Sandbox for development.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All rate/tariff reads fully idempotent. Bill calculation idempotent for same inputs. Pure read API — no state mutations.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth with App ID as username and App Key as password — not standard API key header
US-only coverage — 5,000+ US electric utilities but no international tariff data
LSEID (Load Serving Entity ID) required to identify utility — must look up by zip code or utility name
Tariff data is curated (not real-time) — rates reflect filed tariffs, not real-time prices
Bill calculation API requires usage data (kWh by hour) for accurate TOU calculations
Sandbox and production use different credentials — don't mix environments

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth (App ID + App Key) — weaker than OAuth2. No scope granularity. US data residency. Energy tariff data (not PII). No webhook exposure. Simple credentials — ensure HTTPS always used. Basic auth credentials must be base64-encoded.
